title: Jet Ski Rental Key West: Costs, Seasons & Tips for 2026
html:
Key West offers some of the finest jet ski experiences in the Florida Keys, with departures from Key West Bight Marina and prices from roughly USD 150 for a one-hour rental. BoatHire24 connects you with verified local operators so you can compare options and book securely.

How much does it cost to rent a jet ski in Key West?

Jet ski rentals in Key West typically start around USD 120 per hour and can reach USD 300 for a guided two-hour tour. Shared tours are cheaper, often USD 80, 100 per person for 90 minutes. Private rentals give you more freedom and are ideal for couples or small groups.

For the best rates, consider renting mid-week during the shoulder season. You can compare verified boat rental Key West options on BoatHire24 to find deals that suit your budget.

Where to set off in Key West

Key West Bight Marina is the main hub for jet ski rentals, located on the historic waterfront by William Street. Other departure points include Garrison Bight Marina on the north side, the quieter Cow Key Channel near the airport, and the protected waters of Smathers Beach on the south shore. For longer rides, head out to the Marquesas Keys or the mangrove islands of the backcountry.

Which jet ski to rent

  1. Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski, best for comfort and smooth cruising; seats two; from USD 150 per hour. Check the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski listing for availability.
  2. Yamaha VX Cruiser, ideal for beginners and families; seats three; from USD 120 per hour.
  3. Kawasaki Ultra LX, powerful for exploring further out to Sand Key; seats two; from USD 160 per hour.
  4. Seadoo Spark, lightweight and agile for tricks near the shoreline; seats two; from USD 100 per hour.

Costs at a glance

  • Shared tour: USD 80 per person for 90 minutes
  • Small motorboat (half-day): from USD 250
  • Catamaran full-day charter: from USD 800 for six guests
  • Crewed yacht day charter: from USD 1,500 for eight guests

Do you need a licence to rent a jet ski in Key West?

In Florida, anyone born after 1988 must hold a boater safety card, but jet ski rental operators typically offer a quick safety briefing instead. The exact requirements vary; your operator will confirm what is needed before you set off. For 2026, always check with the rental company as state rules may change.

A local tip: request a map of shallow areas and channel markers to avoid running aground near Woman Key. The prevailing south-east breeze can pick up after noon, so morning sessions often provide flatter water.

Avoid the afternoon winds by booking an early slot from Garrison Bight, the water is calmest before 11am, especially inside the harbour.

At a glance

  • Price band: USD 100, 300 per hour
  • Peak months: December to April and June to August
  • Typical trip length: 1, 2 hours
  • Maximum capacity: 2, 3 persons per jet ski
  • Currency: USD

Questions about jet ski rental in Key West

Can you ride a jet ski from Key West to the Marquesas Keys?

Experienced riders can make the 20-nautical-mile trip, but a guided tour is recommended because of unpredictable currents and shallow patches.

What is the best time of year for jet skiing in Key West?

The winter months of January through April offer cooler air and calm seas, while summer brings higher humidity and a higher chance of afternoon thunderstorms.

Do you need to tip the jet ski guide?

Gratuities are not mandatory but are appreciated; many riders tip 10 to 15 per cent of the rental cost if they receive a good service.

Is insurance included in the rental price?

Most rentals include basic liability insurance, but you may need to pay a damage waiver fee of around USD 30, 50 per rental.

Key West is a year-round destination for jet ski enthusiasts, and BoatHire24 makes it easy to book a rental or even list your boat if you own one. With transparent pricing and verified operators, you can explore the waters confidently.
